criteria.justification: The Component / route / schema breakdown section names concrete Angular components, backend services/controllers, and a new Comment collection, distinguishing new from modified items.
The Frozen API contract section explicitly defines GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE comment endpoints with their request fields, response fields, and auth expectations.

The Edge-case mapping from spec table covers the spec-driven cases around pagination, soft-delete visibility, author-only mutation, membership access, blank submissions, and edits after deletion, and assigns each to a handling layer.
The document stays at design level with named components, models, and endpoint shapes and does not include handler logic or component template code.
It explicitly states the spec was already approved with resolved open questions and does not introduce any new human-facing product decisions beyond that frozen scope.
The breakdown names the dashboard shell, each widget component group, the dashboard controller/query services, and the schema impact, separated into overall and sub-task-specific items.
The Frozen API contract section fully specifies the shared response envelope plus concrete endpoint/request/response shapes for Sub-task A and shape-level contracts for later widget endpoints.
The Session-sizing note explicitly says the dashboard is too large for one backend or frontend session and proposes a three-step sequential split sized around related widget groups.
The output contains only architectural decomposition and data contracts, with no executable route handlers, query code, or UI templates.
The spec status section says the approved dashboard scope is frozen and the plan does not answer new product questions on its own.
